taskDescription: Drag the correct histological terms into the blanks to complete statements about the three types of muscle tissue.
textField:
Skeletal muscle fibres have *multiple* nuclei located at the *peripheral* position beneath the sarcolemma. These fibres are long, cylindrical, and show prominent *cross-striations* due to the regular arrangement of myofilaments.

Cardiac muscle has *central* nuclei, visible cross-striations, and unique *intercalated discs* at cell-to-cell junctions. These discs contain *gap junctions* that allow rapid electrical conduction between adjacent fibres, enabling the heart to function as a *syncytium*.

Smooth muscle cells are *spindle* shaped with a *single* central elongated nucleus and no striations. They are found in the walls of hollow viscera and blood vessels and are innervated by the *autonomic* nervous system.

The basic contractile unit of striated muscle is the *sarcomere*, bounded by *Z lines* on either side. The A band contains thick *myosin* filaments, while the I band contains only thin actin filaments.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the histology of muscle tissue.
Good effort! Review the details you missed.
